channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: MDS incorpora a Adrián Larrondo como nuevo Global Marine director
item.description: MDS ha nombrado a Adrián Larrondo Global Marine Director con el objetivo de reforzar el desarrollo internacional de esa línea de negocio y consolidar su presencia en mercados estratégicos de Iberia y Latinoamérica. Desde Madrid, liderará la estrategia global de la división marítima, impulsando la propuesta de valor para clientes con operaciones internacionales y riesgos complejos. A lo largo de su trayectoria ha ocupado puestos de responsabilidad en firmas como JLT Insurance Brokers, March JLT y Grupo CSAV, desempeñando funciones vinculadas a seguros, siniestros, prevención de pérdidas y asesoramiento legal en Europa, Latinoamérica y Asia-Pacífico.

item.title: El Plan Anual Normativo 2026 refuerza la agenda regulatoria en seguros
item.description: El Ministerio de la Presidencia, Justicia y Relaciones con las Cortes ha publicado el Plan Anual Normativo de la Administración General del Estado para 2026 (PAN 2026) , aprobado recientemente por el Consejo de Ministros. Para 2026, el plan recoge un total de 179 iniciativas, de las que 10 son leyes orgánicas, 38 leyes ordinarias y 131 reales decretos. El plan también incluye medidas con impacto transversal en el sector, como la nueva Ley de Contratos de Crédito al Consumo, la Ley de digitalización y modernización del sector financiero y el desarrollo normativo sobre el uso y gobernanza de la inteligencia artificial.

item.title: El salario ya no es suficiente: las empresas compiten por los expertos en IA con incentivos y flexibilidad
item.description: La escasez de competencias avanzadas en diseño de algoritmos, redes neuronales o modelización de datos está empujando a las empresas a ir más allá del salario fijo para atraer y retener talento. Las empresas combinan cada vez más la mejora salarial con modelos de trabajo flexible, bonus de retención, planes de desarrollo profesional e incentivos a largo plazo para construir propuestas de valor más completas. Las empresas españolas se ven obligadas a replantear sus estrategias de retribución y retención ante la creciente demanda de profesionales en inteligencia artificial, según la ‘Encuesta salarial sobre inteligencia artificial y talento digital’ elaborada por WTW y basado en encuestas salariales en diez mercados internacionales.

item.title: New Zealand Plans Law Change to Stop Private Climate Lawsuits
item.description: New Zealand’s government said on Tuesday it would change climate legislation to prevent courts from finding companies liable in private cases for climate change-related harm caused by greenhouse gas emissions. The New Zealand case, brought by climate change activist Michael Smith against six companies including dairy giant Fonterra Co-Operative Group FCG.NZ, is set to go to trial next year. Government Says Climate Litigation Undermines Business However, Goldsmith said the litigation was undermining business confidence and investment, and that New Zealand’s response to climate change should be managed through parliament, its Emissions Trading Scheme and existing climate legislation.

item.title: AI Tool Predicts Airport Traffic to Avert Devastating Collisions
item.description: Newswise — In managing airport traffic, small errors can cause catastrophe. A group from the CMU Robotics Institute’s AirLab used the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center’s Bridges-2 supercomputer to create World2Rules, an AI that draws from airport data and historical crash reports to help human controllers spot collisions before they happen. On March 12, 2026, an air traffic controller told an Air Canada jet that had just landed at JFK Airport in New York to wait before crossing a runway, to avoid an EVA Air jet that was landing.

item.title: Tesla Premiums Soared in ’25; Loss Ratios 40 Pts. Worse Than Industry: Analysis
item.description: But while Tesla’s premiums soared—particularly in California, where a whopping 585% jump brought the state’s premiums to $725 million, compared to just over $100 million in 2024—loss ratios for one of the Tesla insurers, Tesla Insurance Co., continued to look worse than an industrywide combined ratio in 2025. While auto insurance leaders reported premium growth percentages in the single-digits last year, with sprinters like Progressive and Root up in the low-to-mid teens, premiums at Tesla’s insurance operations skyrocketed 40.7%, according to a new analysis. According to a chart included in the S&P GMI report, the direct loss ratios for Tesla Insurance Co.

item.title: Tasmania presses ahead with scaled-back TasInsure plan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Victoria bushfires claims tally hits 4,700 amid ‘long road to recovery’: ICA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Nepal drought triggers US$100,000 parametric payout to farmers
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: TWIA narrows Alamo Re 2026-1 cat bond size to $725m – $750m, targets low-end pricing
item.description: The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA), the residual market property insurer for the State of Texas, came back to the cat bond market in April with an initial target to secure $450 million of limit from this Alamo Re 2026-1 issuance . This Alamo Re 2026-1 cat bond is set to provide TWIA with reinsurance protection against losses from Texas named storms and severe thunderstorms, on an indemnity trigger and annual aggregate basis, with one tranche of notes having a three-year term, while the other two will run for only two-years. The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA) has now narrowed the targeted size for its new Alamo Re Ltd.

item.title: Travelers targets its largest cat bond sponsorship ever, now up to $750m reinsurance
item.description: Notably, this is now targeted to be Travelers largest catastrophe bond sponsorship ever, making the insurer another company that is increasing its participation in this market, as its risk transfer and reinsurance needs grow. Travelers is the latest company aiming to upsize its catastrophe bond sponsorship in 2026, with the company now seeking between $600 million and $750 million of multi-peril reinsurance protection from the Long Point Re IV Ltd. (Series 2026-1) issuance which would make it the largest cat bond ever sponsored by the insurer, Artemis understands.

item.title: Hiscox Capital Partners wins $1bn mandate from Australian investor TCorp: Report
item.description: Hiscox Capital Partners, the division of Hiscox Re that encompasses insurance-linked securities (ILS) investments and quota-share partnerships, has won a US $1 billion mandate from giant Australian investor TCorp, which is the sovereign investment manager for the New South Wales (NSW) public sector, according to a report. Australian institutional markets publication Financial Standard has reported that TCorp’s head of listed and unlisted asset management James Murray told it about the mandate which has been awarded to Hiscox Capital Partners. We can’t confirm whether the TCorp allocation is the main driver of that increase, of which it was said a meaningful proportion had flowed to the Hiscox Capital Partners catastrophe bond fund strategy, although it feels safe to assume it might be.

item.title: China shielded from immediate supply shock despite Middle East tensions: Coface
item.description: Trade credit insurance specialist Coface says China has so far avoided major supply disruption linked to ongoing tensions in the Middle East, although mounting energy costs are creating fresh pressure across the country’s industrial sector. Despite stable supply flows, Coface says the financial impact of higher energy and chemical prices is becoming increasingly visible throughout the economy. In a new analysis, Coface states that China is in a stronger position than many other Asian economies to withstand prolonged instability in the region.

item.title: Price Forbes names Finn Cackett Joint Managing Director, A&H
item.description: Price Forbes, a specialist insurance and reinsurance broker, has announced the appointment of Finn Cackett as Joint Managing Director, A&H, a London-based role. The executive said: “The Price Forbes A&H team are on an excellent run of form, and spending time with David and the team during the process made it clear why. Cackett, an established figure in the A&H market, will leverage his 30 years of industry experience to develop and execute growth strategies, produce business, and designing new products.

item.title: Accelerant grows Q1’26 exchange premium 16% as member base expands
item.description: Accelerant Holdings has reported its Q1 2026 results, with exchange written premium rising 16% year-on-year to $1.14 billion and adjusted EBITDA increasing by more than 70% as the firm continued scaling its data-driven specialty insurance platform. Accelerant also reported significant growth in membership across its Accelerant Risk Exchange, with the number of members increasing to 296 in Q1 2026 from 232 in Q1 2025. “Exchange Written Premium eclipsed $1 billion for the fourth quarter in a row, growing 16% year-over-year.

item.title: Odey Settles Sexual Assault Cases Ahead of London Trial
item.description: Odey bought an end to the claims ahead of a London trial due to start next month, according to a lawyer for the claimants. Hedge fund boss Crispin Odey has settled a handful of personal injury claims brought by women claiming sexual assault. The accusations ranged in seriousness from an allegation of sexual assault in 2005 to an incident in which Odey forced a receptionist to try on a skirt and show it to him “ostensibly to see if it would fit his daughter,” the FCA said in a legal filing released in February.

item.title: Louisiana Officials Agree to $4.8M Settlement in Death of Black Motorist
item.description: Louisiana officials have agreed to a tentative $4.8 million settlement with the family of Ronald Greene , a Black motorist who died during a violent 2019 roadside arrest carried out by five white officers, two people with knowledge of the agreement told The Associated Press. The settlement would end a federal wrongful death lawsuit filed by the family of Greene, whose death drew national attention after the AP in 2021 obtained footage showing Louisiana State Police officers punching, kicking and using stun guns outside the city of Monroe. The settlement is subject to approval by the Louisiana Legislature, said two people with direct knowledge of the lawsuit who were not authorized to publicly discuss the agreement.

item.title: Dallas Exurb Hits Pause on Data Centers in Rare Texas Rebuke
item.description: “This is a public health, public safety matter that the Commissioners Court needs to take on.” The moratorium amounts to a rare rebuke in growth-friendly Texas as lawmakers and Governor Greg Abbott position the state as a leader in the data center boom. A small Texas county outside Dallas approved a one-year moratorium on new data center and energy storage developments, pushing back against the growing number of large-scale projects fueling the artificial-intelligence boom across the state. Legislators in at least 14 states are considering moratoriums on data centers, and a proposal from Senator Bernie Sanders and US Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez would put data center development on hold nationwide.

item.title: El reto del Buy Now, Pay Later: validar identidades en segundos
item.description: En tiempos de inmediatez, scrolling y tap tap, el modelo Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) enfrenta el reto de validar la identidad, analizar el riesgo y aprobar líneas de crédito en segundos y con la menor fricción posible. Todo en un entorno donde cualquier demora o exceso de pasos pueden afectar la conversión. “Como equipo de seguridad, tenemos que ser muy eficientes y ágiles para que siempre los nuevos controles y las nuevas cosas que hacemos estén dedicadas al negocio”, explica a iupana Héctor Gutiérrez, director de seguridad de información en Kueski, una fintech mexicana dedicada a créditos BNPL.

item.title: Tokio Marine open to more ILS deals ‘if there’s any room’, as Kizuna Re highlights strong earthquake demand
item.description: Please Login The post Tokio Marine open to more ILS deals ‘if there’s any room’, as Kizuna Re highlights strong earthquake demand appeared first on InsuranceAsia News . Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: Cerebras Sees Sizzling Demand For IPO Shares
item.description: Cerebras Systems priced shares for its upcoming IPO at $185 a piece late Wednesday — well above the projected range of $150 to $160 — raising at least $5.55 billion for the company and valuing it at $56.4 billion, according to a CNBC report . Shares of the company, which develops AI computing chips and large-scale AI systems, are slated to begin trading on Nasdaq Thursday under the ticker symbol CBRS. The IPO has been a long time coming for Sunnyvale, California-based Cerebras, which initially filed publicly for an IPO in September, 2024.

item.title: Arity, Experian Partner on Driving Data Access
item.description: Arity (Chicago) and Experian (Dublin, Ireland) have partnered to make consumer-consented driving behavior data available to auto insurers through Experian’s insurance marketplace. The partnership connects Arity’s driving intelligence with Experian’s insurance shopping platform, allowing carriers to incorporate driving behavior into quote and qualification workflows. Arity says its analytics are based on driving behavior data tied to auto insurance claims.

item.title: CommScope Sued by Lenders for at Least $150 Million Over Alleged Breach
item.description: was sued by a group of lenders who allege the telecommunications provider breached a debt agreement by failing to pay a prescribed premium of at least $150 million following a key asset sale. Such a breach requires CommScope to pay a premium to lenders, according to the court filing dated Tuesday in the New York state court. The lenders contend that CommScope’s January divestiture of its broadband connectivity and cable unit for $10.5 billion to Amphenol Corp, constituted a sale of substantially all of it assets and therefore triggered an event of default under the terms governing its debt.

item.title: U.S. Indicts 2 Foreign Operators, Individual in 2024 Baltimore Key Bridge Collapse
item.description: Justice Department said on Tuesday that a grand jury indicted two foreign operators and a shoreside superintendent in the March 2024 collision of the 984-foot cargo ship Dali that destroyed Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge and killed six construction workers. According to the indictment, the Dali lost power twice in four minutes, as it was leaving the Port of Baltimore, causing it to crash into the Key Bridge. The Dali’s operators — Synergy Marine Pte Ltd, based in Singapore, and Synergy Maritime Pte Ltd based in Chennai, India along with Radhakrishnan Karthik Nair, 47, an Indian national who worked for both firms as the technical superintendent for the Dali, are charged with conspiracy, willfully failing to immediately inform the U.S.

item.title: Driver Rescinds Guilty Plea in Deadly Wrong-way Crash into LA Sheriff’s Recruits
item.description: A driver has rescinded his guilty plea on charges related to a wrong-way crash that led to the death of a Los Angeles sheriff’s recruit and injured other recruits who were out on a run, his lawyer said Saturday. Nicholas Gutierrez of Diamond Bar, withdrew his plea after more than a dozen crash victims spoke in court Friday and the judge decided to sentence him to a year in jail, lawyer Alexandra Kazarian said. Authorities have said Gutierrez was driving an SUV that veered onto the wrong side of the road in suburban Whittier in 2022, striking 25 recruits who were out on a morning run.

item.title: India’s insurance premiums grow 9.86% to US$83.8bn by November: IRDAI
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Anduril Raises Another $5B As Defense Tech Startups Shatter Funding Records
item.description: Defense tech startup Anduril Industries said Wednesday that it has raised another $5 billion in funding at a $61 billion valuation — double the valuation of $30.5 billion it received less than a year ago. After Anduril, several other defense-tech startups, all based in the U.S., have received sizable investments this year: Stay up to date with recent funding rounds, acquisitions, and more with the Crunchbase Daily. The funding comes amid record venture investment into startups developing defense, wartime and national security technologies and a Trump administration push to modernize the U.S.

item.title: PERILS raises storm Nils insured market loss estimate 31% to €767m
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has updated its industry loss estimate for extratropical windstorm Nils, also known as Ulrike, that battered the Nouvelle-Aquitaine and Occitanie regions in southwest France in mid-February, lifting the total almost 31% to €767 million. PERILS came out with its initial insurance industry loss estimate for this windstorm back in March, putting the total at €586 million. PERILS noted that its loss estimate is based on data gathered from impacted insurers, including losses from property and motor lines of business.

item.title: Aon expands Global ReSpecialty division with Mitchell and Rimmer appointments
item.description: Aon, the global insurance and reinsurance broking group, has announced that it has appointed both Jimmy Mitchell and Adam Rimmer as brokers within the firm’s Global ReSpecialty team, effective immediately. Aon’s Global ReSpecialty division provides specialty reinsurance and retrocession solutions, often engaging with insurance-linked securities (ILS) and collateralized markets on behalf of their client base. Mitchell has been appointed Vice President of Global ReSpecialty, a New York-based role.

item.title: American Integrity’s GPW climb to $220m in Q1’26 as policies in-force reach 437k
item.description: American Integrity Insurance Group, Inc., a Tampa-based property and casualty insurance holding company, has reported gross premiums written (GPW) of $220 million for the first quarter of 2026, up 3.7% from $212.2 million in the same period a year earlier. American Integrity posted a combined ratio of 75.0%, compared to 42.9% in the first quarter of 2025. As of March 31, 2026, total policies in-force were 437,308, an increase of 14.1% compared to 383,332 as of March 31, 2025.

item.title: Jimmy Mitchell and Adam Rimmer join Aon’s Global ReSpecialty team
item.description: Global re/insurance broker and risk advisor Aon has announced the appointments of Jimmy Mitchell and Adam Rimmer as brokers in the firm’s Global ReSpecialty team, effective immediately. Richard Wheeler, co-leader of Global ReSpecialty, commented: “We are excited to welcome Jimmy and Adam to our growing team to help drive better decision making for our clients. Mitchell has been named Vice President of Global ReSpecialty, a New York-based role.

item.title: We’re identifying key clients in casualty as part of measured medium-term strategy, says Conduit Re CEO
item.description: Bermuda-based, global reinsurer Conduit Re grew its casualty book by more than 23% in the first quarter of 2026, and was able to expand its share with existing clients and develop new relationships as the company embraces its medium-term strategy, according to Neil Eckert, Chief Executive Officer (CEO). But as I say, that’s part of the measured medium-term strategy,” said Eckert. “The principal area of growth for us was casualty, and we do target certain preferred clients where we want to enter into medium and long-term partnerships.

item.title: UAE-Owned Tanker Leaks Some Fuel Off Oman Following Iranian Strike
item.description: A unit of state-owned Abu Dhabi National Oil Company said on Wednesday that one of its tankers struck by Iranian drones last week has leaked a small amount of fuel off the coast of Oman, underscoring the ecological risks stemming from the Iran war. Barakah, and was working “closely with the relevant authorities and specialist response teams.” Read more: US $20B Reinsurance Plan Unlikely to Restart Gulf Shipping Without Liability Cover “The ADNOC Logistics & Services vessel Barakah remains at anchor off the coast of Oman after being impacted by two Iranian drones on May 4,” a company spokesperson said. “A small amount of what is understood to be bunker fuel was unfortunately released as a result of the incident.” The spokesperson did not say how much fuel was believed to have leaked.

item.title: Former Walgreens Manager Sentenced for Role in 7 Inside-Job Robberies
item.description: A District of Columbia woman was sentenced last week to 32 months in prison for her role in a series of seven inside-job robberies of the Chinatown drug store where she was employed as a store manager, reported U.S. According to court documents, Teeter and three co-conspirators carried out armed robberies of the Walgreens store in Chinatown nearly once a month, beginning in July 2023, when either she or her co-conspirator were working. According to prosecutors, Teeter and her co-conspirator took turns pretending to be the victim manager on duty, knowing that the robberies would be captured on internal surveillance.

item.title: South Dakota Drilling Project Canceled After Backlash From Tribes
item.description: A South Dakota mining company has canceled a drilling project in the Black Hills after opposition from Native American tribes and local groups. Nine tribes in South Dakota, North Dakota and Nebraska filed a lawsuit against the Forest Service over the project alleging violations of the National Historical Preservation Act and National Environmental Protection Act for granting permits without an environmental review. In a letter provided Friday by Indigenous advocacy group NDN Collective, Rapid City-based Pete Lien & Sons told the United States Forest Service on Thursday it is withdrawing its plan of operations for a graphite drilling project.

item.title: Allianz posts record €4.5 billion operating profit in Q1 2026
item.description: Allianz reported record operating profit of €4.5 billion for the first quarter of 2026, as strong underwriting performance in property and casualty insurance and continued growth in asset management helped offset a slight decline in total business volume. Operating profit for the division reached a record €2.4 billion, an increase of 11.1% year over year, as underwriting profitability improved and insurance service results strengthened. Allianz reaffirmed its full-year operating profit target of €17.4 billion, plus or minus €1 billion, and confirmed that its previously announced €2.5 billion share buyback program remains underway.

item.title: Aon expands Claims Copilot platform across global markets
item.description: The company plans to continue expanding Claims Copilot into additional EMEA and Latin American markets over the coming months as it further embeds the platform within its global Commercial Risk operations. Aon has expanded its Claims Copilot platform across North America, Asia Pacific, and parts of Europe, the Middle East, and Africa as the firm continues investing in AI-driven analytics and connected claims management capabilities. The rollout follows a pilot programme launched in late 2025 and represents a broader effort by Aon to centralise claims operations and improve data visibility across its global commercial risk business.

item.title: Insurance Industry Faces Critical Talent Shortage
item.description: As 400,000 insurance professionals retire and Gen Z stays away, workforce gaps are becoming critical operational risks. The insurance industry is one built on history and resilience, but it's also one where the future is facing immense uncertainty. And yet, 79% say they've never considered working in insurance due to perceptions of the industry being "boring" or too corporate.

item.title: Samsung Fire rolls out autonomous vehicle insurance
item.description: Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance is launching coverage for autonomous vehicles in South Korea. Moving forward, as a reliable partner in the changes of the mobility industry, we will realize the establishment of a social safety net in addition to fulfilling the fundamental role of insurance in protecting customers’ daily lives.” – Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance. Beyond accidents caused by the negligence of manufacturers, software developers, and vehicle controllers, the insurance will also protect against cyber security risks resulting from external hacking.

item.title: Inszone acquires Arkansas agency Smith & Company
item.description: Inszone Insurance announced the acquisition of Smith & Company Insurance, an Arkansas agency with roots dating back to 1904. The agency, owned by Mark Smith, Drew Higginbothom, and William Smith, offers commercial, personal, and benefits insurance, with specialties in aviation and commercial agriculture. Smith & Company also writes policies for multiple banks in the region.

item.title: AXA XL launches business unit to scale prevention services
item.description: AXA XL has launched a dedicated Prevention Business Unit, marking a strategic expansion of its risk consulting and prevention capabilities as the insurer responds to growing client demand for proactive risk management services. Announced Wednesday, the new division becomes AXA XL’s fifth business unit alongside Americas, APAC & Europe, UK & Lloyd’s, and Reinsurance. According to AXA XL, the new business unit will combine advanced analytics, technology, and risk engineering expertise to help clients address increasingly interconnected threats, including climate-related risks, cyber exposure, supply chain disruption, and operational resilience challenges.

item.title: Lawsuit Claims OpenAI Chatbot Gave Advice That Led to Fatal Overdose
item.description: The lawsuit seeks monetary damages and asks the court to pause OpenAI’s rollout of ChatGPT Health, a platform the company announced in January that allows users to upload medical records and receive personalized health advice. The parents of a man who died of an accidental drug overdose last year sued OpenAI and its founder and CEO Sam Altman in a California court on Tuesday, alleging the man was coached to take a dangerous combination of substances by ChatGPT. Related: Family of FSU Mass Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI The combination of those drugs and alcohol resulted in Nelson’s death in May 2025, according to the lawsuit, which was filed in state court in San Francisco.

item.title: India launches US$1.5bn maritime insurance pool, issues first war risk policies
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Axa XL launches prevention unit, led by global CUO Libby Benet
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA reviews whether investment firms are doing enough to support bereaved customers
item.description: The FCA is reviewing how consumer investment firms support bereaved customers and whether they're getting it right. Fewer than half of bereaved customers (47%) felt they received the support they needed from financial firms, according to research (PDF) . It will assess how firms communicate, how they support vulnerable customers, their service standards, and how fees are handled on bereaved accounts.

item.title: FCA announces new appointments to executive team
item.description: The FCA has announced 2 permanent appointments to its executive team, strengthening leadership at a pivotal time for UK and global financial markets. As the leading and largest European regulator, the FCA leads from the front in many regulated areas by protecting customers, driving smarter regulation, and combatting financial crime through collaboration and the use of AI.' Nikhil Rathi, chief executive of the FCA said: 'A strong leadership team is a crucial part of us being the smarter regulator we aspire to be. Simon Walls has been appointed permanent executive director, markets.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Agtech Startups Face A Drier Funding Climate
item.description: The broad trend: Venture funding in agriculture and farming is still undergoing a market correction following the hyper-funding era of 2021, when startups in the space raised $10.5 billion across 1,419 deals, a review of Crunchbase data shows. Many agtech startups that raised massive rounds during that 2021 peak have struggled to show real-world traction. The numbers: So far this year through May 7, agtech startups have raised $1.4 billion, on track to be down slightly or on par with the $4.4 billion raised last year and the $4.6 billion raised in 2024.

item.title: Exclusive: Physician-Founded Saile Raises $2.2M To Help Doctors Find Side Jobs Using AI
item.description: Marc Ayoub , a neurosurgeon born into a family of doctors, the path to entrepreneurship was a necessity born of frustration. “The bottleneck is not the number of doctors, but the fragmented infrastructure connecting them to where they are needed,” said Ayoub, who also serves as an assistant professor of neurosurgery at the Donald & Barbara Zucker School of Medicine. “Most people assume the issue in healthcare staffing is a lack of doctors, but what we’ve seen is something different.

item.title: Northern Re expands use of Ultrassure platform to support reinsurance contract operations
item.description: Northern Re, the collateralized reinsurance company that provides investors with access to the returns of its long-tail casualty underwriting business, has expanded its use of Ultrassure’s platform from an initial pilot to production deployment for contract workflows across its reinsurance operations. During the pilot, Northern Re and Ultrassure reportedly worked together to refine the platform’s fit for day-to-day contract work, with a particular focus on usability, responsiveness to feedback, and operational readiness. The production rollout also reflects Northern Re’s choice to transition the platform into active operation following its evaluation in live workflows and the analysis of its potential to enhance contract operations with improved consistency, efficiency, and control.

item.title: Mangrove now aims for up to $111m of named storm reinsurance from Buttonwood Re cat bond
item.description: Now, we’re told that this debut cat bond under Buttonwood Re now targets between the initial $100 million and up to $111 million of named storm reinsurance protection in the state of Florida for Mangrove. Mangrove Property Insurance is now aiming to secure up to $111 million of named storm reinsurance protection from its first Buttonwood Re Ltd. For its first catastrophe bond sponsorship under Buttonwood Re, Mangrove is aiming to secure both occurrence and aggregate reinsurance to protect its business against losses from named storms and hurricanes in the state of Florida.
